response:
Problems found:

1. Missing imports  
   The function uses os and json but never imports them.  
   Symptom: NameError at runtime.

2. Predictable / world-writable file name  
   File name is always /tmp/data_<pid>.json.  
   • Anyone who can write to /tmp can pre-create a symlink with that name and
     make your code overwrite /etc/passwd or any other file (symlink-race /
     TOCTOU vulnerability).  
   • Inside the same process, a second call overwrites the first file.  
   • A forked child shares the same PID on some systems (e.g. containers) and
     may clobber the file as well.

3. No error handling / cleanup  
   If json.dump raises (e.g. unserializable object) the half-written file is
   left behind.  
   Caller never knows it failed.

4. File permissions depend on the process’ umask  
   On many systems the file will be 0666 & ~umask.  A lax umask (e.g. 0002,
   sometimes 0022) makes the file readable by other users, which can be a
   data-leak.

5. Hard-coded “/tmp”  
   • Not portable to Windows.  
   • A chroot or container might want a different temp directory.

6. Not truly “temporary”  
   The function never removes the file; the disk fills up unless the caller
   remembers to delete it.

7. Minor style issues  
   Manual string concatenation instead of os.path.join / f-string; no encoding
   specified; the function returns a path only, not the open handle, which may
   force the caller to reopen the file.

How to fix

• Use the standard tempfile module.  It gives you an unpredictable name, opens
  the file with O_CREAT|O_EXCL (no race), applies 0600 permissions, and is
  cross-platform.

Example improved implementation:

```python
import json
import os
import tempfile

def create_temp_file(data, *, dir=None, prefix='data_', suffix='.json'):
    """
    Serialise *data* to a secure temporary JSON file.
    Returns the path.  Caller is responsible for deleting it.
    """
    # mkstemp -> (fd, path).  O_EXCL ensures no symlink race.
    fd, path = tempfile.mkstemp(prefix=prefix, suffix=suffix, dir=dir, text=True)
    try:
        with os.fdopen(fd, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
            json.dump(data, f, ensure_ascii=False)
            f.flush()
            os.fsync(f.fileno())          # optional: force to disk
    except Exception:
        os.unlink(path)                   # remove partial file on error
        raise
    return path
```

Optional extras

• If you want the OS to delete the file automatically when closed, use
  tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(delete=True) and return the open handle
  instead of a path.

• If the data may be sensitive, consider encrypting it at rest or storing it
  in an in-memory temp file (io.BytesIO) instead of on disk.

Summary

– import the required modules  
– use tempfile.mkstemp / NamedTemporaryFile instead of a predictable path  
– handle exceptions and clean up  
– rely on 0600 permissions or set them explicitly  
– let the temp directory be configurable / platform-independent  
– optionally ensure automatic deletion and fsync when needed
